comment_text.sn5.3:1.2: Regarded as the foremost of those who wear coarse robes (AN 1.246:1.1), she has a different set of verses at Thig 10.1:0.3.
comment_text.sn5.3:2.4: Māra evidently believes that a grieving mother’s first priority is to seek a lover in a dark forest.
comment_text.sn5.3:5.4: She addresses Māra as āvuso.

root_text.sn5.3:1.2: Atha kho kisāgotamī bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.sn5.3:1.3: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena andhavanaṁ tenupasaṅkami, upasaṅkamitvā divāvihārāya.
root_text.sn5.3:1.4: Andhavanaṁ ajjhogāhetvā aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle divāvihāraṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn5.3:1.5: Atha kho māro pāpimā kisāgotamiyā bhikkhuniyā bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo samādhimhā cāvetukāmo yena kisāgotamī bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā kisāgotamiṁ bhikkhuniṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn5.3:3.2: “ko nu khvāyaṁ manusso vā amanusso vā gāthaṁ bhāsatī”ti?
root_text.sn5.3:3.4: “māro kho ayaṁ pāpimā mama bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo samādhimhā cāvetukāmo gāthaṁ bhāsatī”ti.
root_text.sn5.3:4.1: Atha kho kisāgotamī bhikkhunī “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāhi paccabhāsi:
root_text.sn5.3:7.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ kisāgotamī bhikkhunī”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn5.3:1.2: Then the nun Kisāgotamī robed up in the morning and, taking her bowl and robe, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.sn5.3:1.3: She wandered for alms in Sāvatthī. After the meal, on her return from almsround, she went to the Dark Forest for the day’s meditation,
translation_text.sn5.3:1.4: plunged deep into it, and sat at the root of a tree to meditate.
translation_text.sn5.3:1.5: Then Māra the Wicked, wanting to make the nun Kisāgotamī feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, wanting to make her fall away from immersion, went up to her and addressed her in verse:
translation_text.sn5.3:2.1: “Why do you sit alone and cry
translation_text.sn5.3:2.2: as if your children have died?
translation_text.sn5.3:2.3: You’ve come to the woods all alone—
translation_text.sn5.3:2.4: are you looking for a man?”
translation_text.sn5.3:3.2: “Who’s speaking this verse, a human or a non-human?”
translation_text.sn5.3:3.4: “This is Māra the Wicked, wanting to make me feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, wanting to make me fall away from immersion!”
translation_text.sn5.3:4.1: Then Kisāgotamī, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, replied to him in verse:
translation_text.sn5.3:5.1: “I’ve got over the death of children,
translation_text.sn5.3:5.4: and I’m not afraid of you, sir!
translation_text.sn5.3:6.1: Relishing is banished in every respect,
translation_text.sn5.3:6.2: and the mass of darkness is shattered.
translation_text.sn5.3:6.3: I’ve defeated the army of death,
translation_text.sn5.3:7.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The nun Kisāgotamī knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.

reference_text.sn5.3:1.1: bj13.236, csp1ed12.131, csp2ed12.131, ms12S1_951, msdiv164, ndp12.130, pts-vp-pli1ed1.130, pts-vp-pli2ed1.285
